mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate, even in the presence of inflation, indicates an accommodative monetary policy that supports equity valuations by lowering the discount rate, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite low FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and broader economic slowdown, which typically precedes or accompanies a decline in corporate earnings and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 60d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is strong but accompanied by high and accelerating inflation, it indicates an overheating economy that will likely trigger aggressive monetary tightening, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:overheating economies lead to Fed tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances where both retail sales and inflation are high and accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, sustained high GDP growth indicates strong corporate fundamentals and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This effect diminishes in recessionary periods.
evidence_refs: fact:strong GDP growth supports corporate earnings
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion (GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized for 2 consecutive quarters)
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 60d returns during periods of sustained high GDP growth in an expansionary regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates a significant slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic demand, signaling a potential contraction that negatively impacts short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong non-farm payrolls growth indicates a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20d returns during periods of sustained strong PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high and rising consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and increases the likelihood of aggressive monetary tightening, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon of 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation often leads to Fed tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 60d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is declining while the unemployment rate is rising, it signals a severe economic downturn with weakening demand and supply, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average change < -0.5% AND UNRATE 3-month average change > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:simultaneous decline in production and rise in unemployment indicates recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 20d returns when both industrial production is declining and unemployment is rising.
